alerts.name: Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens
alerts.desc: No Anti-CSRF tokens were found in a HTML submission form. A CSRF attack forces a logged-on victim's browser to send a forged HTTP request to a vulnerable web application.
alerts.solution: Use the OWASP CSRF Guard or a similar library. Include a unique anti-CSRF token in every form.

alerts.name: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
alerts.desc: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is a client-side attack where the attacker injects malicious script that runs in the victim's browser. Found in the q parameter of the search page.
alerts.solution: HTML-encode user input before reflecting it. Use a templating engine with auto-escaping. Set Content-Security-Policy headers.

alerts.alert: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alerts.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alerts.desc: CSP is an effective measure to protect from XSS attacks. By whitelisting sources of approved content, attacks are mitigated.
alerts.solution: Configure the server to send a Content-Security-Policy header on responses.
